location.description: Where the error occurred, e.g. 'body.items[3].tags' or 'path.thing-id'

value.description: The value at the given location

$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
examples: Property foo is required but is missing.

errors.description: Optional list of individual error details

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. This value should not change between occurrences of the error.

type.description: A URI reference to human-readable documentation for the error.

get.description: Lists API keys for the caller's organization with simple limit/offset paging. Admin only. Use POST /api-keys/list for richer filters (search, created_by, sort).

post.description: Mints a fresh API key for the caller's organization. Admin only. The plaintext key value is returned exactly once and never stored or returned again.
Lists API keys with full filter and sort options in the request body. Admin only.

delete.description: Revokes an API key. Subsequent requests using its value are rejected as unauthorized. Admin only.

post.description: Rotates the plaintext value for an existing API key, preserving its name and identifier. The previous value is invalidated immediately. Admin only.
Creates a hosted checkout session for the org to top up its credit balance. Admin only.
post.summary: Start a checkout session to add funds

get.description: Returns the organization's current credit balance in microdollars plus a display string.

post.description: Returns a temporary URL into the hosted billing portal for managing payment methods, invoices, etc. Admin only.

get.description: Paginated invoice history for the caller's organization with optional filters and sort.

parameters.description: RFC3339 upper bound for invoice_date (with date_from = an exact calendar month)
parameters.name: date_to

schema.description: RFC3339 upper bound for invoice_date (with date_from = an exact calendar month)

parameters.description: filter to invoices for a dedicated phone
parameters.name: phone_id

schema.description: filter to invoices for a dedicated phone

post.description: Returns the PDF URL for an invoice the caller's org owns.
post.summary: Get a download URL for an invoice

post.description: Refreshes this org's invoice history from the payment provider. Admin only.
Reserves a shared device and creates a hosted checkout session for the rental subscription. Admin only.
post.summary: Start a checkout session for a device rental

get.description: Returns every active and pending device rental subscription owned by the caller's organization.

post.description: Marks each listed rental subscription to cancel at period end. Admin only.

get.description: Returns one filtered, sorted page of the caller organization's active device rental subscriptions, plus an org-wide summary (active count, combined monthly spend, per-interval breakdown, and upcoming charges).

post.description: Reverses a pending cancel-at-period-end on each listed rental subscription. Admin only.

get.description: Returns the active billing subscription for the caller's organization.

post.description: Marks the active subscription to cancel at the end of the current billing period. Admin only.
Creates a hosted checkout session for upgrading to a paid plan. Admin only.
post.summary: Start a checkout session for a plan

delete.description: Rolls back a pending downgrade so the org stays on the current plan at period end. Admin only.

post.description: Schedules a plan downgrade to take effect at period end. Validates the change first. Admin only.

get.description: Computes the impact (limit changes, blockers, warnings, effective date) of switching to plan_id. Admin only.
get.summary: Validate a plan change before executing

get.description: Returns every organization the caller is a member of.

post.description: Creates an organization. Idempotent by the linked organization id so retries and duplicate POSTs converge. The calling user becomes the org's first admin.

get.description: Returns a single organization the caller is a member of.

patch.description: Applies a partial update (name, slug, logo). Also marks onboarding complete the first time name or slug is set. Org admin only.

get.description: Returns every outstanding invitation for the organization. Org admin only.

post.description: Creates an organization invitation. Idempotent by org_id + email: rejects 409 on duplicate. Org admin only.
post.summary: Invite a user to an organization

delete.description: Revokes an outstanding invitation and deletes the local record. Org admin only.

get.description: Returns every member of the organization with their role and email.

post.description: Adds a user as a member of the organization with the given role. Idempotent: rejects 409 if the user is already a member. Org admin only.

delete.description: Removes a user from the organization. Rejects removing yourself, and rejects removing the last admin of an organization. Org admin only.

patch.description: Changes a member's role. Rejects changing your own role, and rejects downgrading the last admin. Org admin only.

parameters.description: user identifier whose role is being changed
parameters.name: user_id

schema.description: user identifier whose role is being changed

post.description: Allocates a device to a workflow from the editor. If allocation setup fails it is rolled back so the user can't be billed for a session that never starts.
post.summary: Allocate a phone to a workflow

get.description: Returns whether the given workflow currently holds an active device allocation, including the session_id when present. The optional allocated_by filter narrows results to allocations originating from a specific context (workflow_editor / scheduler).

parameters.description: optional: only consider allocations made via this context (workflow_editor/scheduler)
parameters.name: allocated_by

schema.description: optional: only consider allocations made via this context (workflow_editor/scheduler)

get.summary: Check if a workflow has an active device

get.description: Returns ACTIVE unallocated devices the caller's org can claim, optionally filtered by device type (iphone/android). Counts by type are included alongside the list.
Returns per-location capacity (available + max) across the device fleet. Powers the location picker in the workflow editor.

post.description: Initializes a WebRTC session for the connected device. Rolls back the underlying allocation if session setup fails.
post.summary: Connect a device to a workflow session

get.description: Returns device counts grouped by type (iphone/android) for the given status (ACTIVE/INACTIVE/MAINTENANCE).
get.summary: Count devices by type for a status

get.description: Returns the apps the platform supports orchestration for, optionally filtered by platform + category. Platform admins see internal/unreleased apps too.
Returns private and rented devices owned by the caller's org. include_expired=true keeps rentals past their rental_expires_at in the result so users can see what they used to own.

parameters.description: include rented devices whose rental window has expired
parameters.name: include_expired

schema.description: include rented devices whose rental window has expired

parameters.description: free-text search across nickname, name, model, location
parameters.name: search

schema.description: free-text search across nickname, name, model, location

parameters.description: only phones whose rental expires at/after this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: rental_expires_after

schema.description: only phones whose rental expires at/after this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only phones whose rental expires at/before this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: rental_expires_before

schema.description: only phones whose rental expires at/before this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only phones last seen at/after this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: last_active_after

schema.description: only phones last seen at/after this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only phones last seen at/before this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: last_active_before

schema.description: only phones last seen at/before this RFC3339 time

get.summary: List the caller's private + rented devices

get.description: Returns one page of the org's phone sessions for the Session Inspector table: active/unbilled sessions pinned on top, terminal history paginated beneath. Covers workflow runs and workflow-less interactive leases; each row links to a session. Filters: search, workflow_id, status.

parameters.description: filter by source: workflow and/or interactive; repeatable
parameters.name: source

schema.description: filter by source: workflow and/or interactive; repeatable

parameters.description: filter by type: shared and/or dedicated; repeatable
parameters.name: dedicated

schema.description: filter by type: shared and/or dedicated; repeatable

parameters.description: only sessions started at/after this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: started_after

schema.description: only sessions started at/after this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only sessions started at/before this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: started_before

schema.description: only sessions started at/before this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only sessions de-allocated at/after this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: ended_after

schema.description: only sessions de-allocated at/after this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only sessions de-allocated at/before this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: ended_before

schema.description: only sessions de-allocated at/before this RFC3339 time

get.description: Returns one page of the organization's currently-active phone sessions joined with phone + workflow display fields: in-flight runs, workflow-less interactive leases, and dedicated phones in use. Paginated via limit (default 25, max 100) + offset; the response total is the full active count. Powers the dashboard overview.

parameters.description: case-insensitive match on phone name/nickname/id, session id, or workflow name
parameters.name: search

schema.description: case-insensitive match on phone name/nickname/id, session id, or workflow name

get.summary: List the org's active phone allocations

get.description: Returns one session for the Session Inspector: session lifecycle + phone display fields + workflow name (when tied to one) + an inlined presigned recording URL. Works for active and terminal sessions, and for workflow runs and workflow-less interactive leases. Org-scoped: another org's session reads as not found.

Returns a short-lived URL for the session's screen recording, keyed on session_id — so it works for workflow runs and workflow-less interactive leases alike. Status is "pending" (no URL) when the recording hasn't finished uploading yet. Org-scoped: another org's session reads as not found.
get.summary: Get a session's screen recording URL

post.description: Deallocates a device the caller's org currently holds. The session is billed and the device is torn down asynchronously.

get.description: Returns the caller's private devices currently eligible for an editor allocation (private + active + not allocated), optionally filtered by device type.
get.summary: List the caller's devices eligible for workflow allocation

get.description: Returns a single device by its identifier.

post.description: Sends an imperative command to the device and waits for the device to acknowledge its result.
post.summary: Send a command to a phone and wait for its result

post.description: Bumps the device's last_interaction_timestamp. Powers the idle-timeout cron and the dashboard's "last active" column.

patch.description: Sets the human-readable display name on a private device the caller's org owns. Returns the updated device summary.

post.description: Requests an on-demand factory reset of a private device the caller's org owns. Requires the device to be ACTIVE and not currently allocated. Sets the device to MAINTENANCE while the wipe is carried out.
post.summary: Wipe a private device on demand

post.description: Returns paginated recent runs for the caller's org. Filters: workflow, search text, status, trigger; sort by any of the columns listed in RunSortField.
Returns paginated run events for a session, filtered by session_id.
Returns paginated historic runs for the caller's user. Use POST /runs for recent (non-archived) runs.

get.description: Returns total run count + success rate for the given workflow, scoped to the caller's user.
get.summary: Get aggregate stats for a workflow

get.description: Returns one run by ID, scoped to the caller's organization.

patch.description: Transitions a run to CANCELLED, scoped to the caller's org.

post.description: Creates one or more runs against the given workflow and queues them for execution. Pre-flight checks: balance sufficient, concurrency limit, workflow exists. Runs that fail to queue are marked FAILED immediately so they stop counting toward the concurrency limit.
Paginated, filterable list of inference calls (/infer + /locate) the caller's user was billed for. Filters: date range, endpoint, free-text search. Ordered by call time DESC.

get.description: Returns infrastructure cost and compute-minute summaries for the caller's user over a date range, plus per-bucket chart data. Granularity is hourly (≤24h window) or daily. Use POST /usage/metrics if you need richer body params; this endpoint takes query params only.

parameters.description: IANA timezone for bucketing periods (e.g., America/Los_Angeles)
parameters.name: timezone

schema.description: IANA timezone for bucketing periods (e.g., America/Los_Angeles)

post.description: Body-based variant of GET /usage/metrics for clients that prefer JSON over query strings.

Paginated, filterable list of phone sessions for the caller's user. Filters: date range, session status, processed status, workflow, allocator, free-text search. Sort: first entry of sort_by; defaults to allocated_at DESC.

get.description: Returns the caller's masked OpenAI + Claude API key presence. Stored keys are encrypted at rest; the response shows only the last four characters.

put.description: Applies a partial update to the caller's API key settings. A nil field leaves the column unchanged; an empty string clears it; a value replaces it. Writes fail with a 500 if the server-side encryption key is not configured.

delete.description: Deletes the caller's organizations (best-effort) then their user account. Account data cleanup happens asynchronously via identity-provider webhooks.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile (id, email, verified/admin flags, timestamps).
Paginated list of workflows in the caller's org. Filters via query params: search, status, platform. Use POST /workflows/list for richer body-shaped queries with sort.

parameters.description: only workflows created at/after this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: created_after

schema.description: only workflows created at/after this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only workflows created at/before this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: created_before

schema.description: only workflows created at/before this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only workflows last run at/after this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: last_run_after

schema.description: only workflows last run at/after this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only workflows last run at/before this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: last_run_before

schema.description: only workflows last run at/before this RFC3339 time

post.description: Creates a new workflow in the caller's org. Name must match ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$ and be unique within the org. Returns the workflow_id.

Creates a workflow and its first code revision in a single transaction — the code playground's 'save as workflow'. Name must match ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$ and be unique within the org; platform defaults to ANDROID. Returns the workflow_id and revision_id.
post.summary: Create a workflow from a code buffer (atomic)

post.description: Paginated list of workflows in the caller's org with full filter + sort options in the request body.

delete.description: Deletes a workflow. Org-scoped — workflows in other orgs return 404.

get.description: Returns a single workflow, scoped to the caller's org (workflows in other orgs return 404).

put.description: Applies a partial update (name, platform, status, ocr_engine). Org-scoped — workflows in other orgs return 404.

get.description: Returns the source code of the workflow's current revision, scoped to the caller's org.

post.description: Persists a new revision of the workflow's code. Hash-deduplicates against the current revision (no-op if unchanged). Source is capped at 256KB.
post.summary: Save workflow code (creates a new revision)

post.description: Creates a new revision with the source of the named revision. Does NOT dedup against the current revision so the action is auditable in the revision history.

get.description: Returns revision metadata (id, number, author, message, bytes, sha256, created_at) for the workflow, in reverse-chronological order. Use the `before` cursor to paginate older revisions.

parameters.description: cursor: return revisions older than this revision number
parameters.name: before

schema.description: cursor: return revisions older than this revision number

get.description: Returns a single revision including its full source. Defense-in-depth: the revision must belong to the named workflow or it's treated as missing.

patch.description: Bumps the workflow's last_run_at timestamp after a run starts.
